'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Tsukiyomi-jinja (Iki)}} [[JA:月讀神社 (壱岐市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11516219}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Tsukiyomi Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:Iki Tukiyomi shrine.JPG|250px|Tsukiyomi Shrine]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|Q169376|WD=Q169376}}{{ill|lt=Iki|Q861519|WD=Q861519}} Ashibe-chō Kokubuhigashi-fure 464 |位置 = {{Coord|33|47|58.69|N|129|43|25.35|E|region:JP-42_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsukiyomi Shrine}} |祭神 = Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto
Tsukuyumi-no-Mikoto
Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto |社格 = candidate shrine for Shikinaisha major shrine (myōjin major) / former unranked shrine |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = October 23 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagasaki|label=Tsukiyomi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[ファイル:Iki tsukiyomishrine haiden.jpg|サムネイル|Distant view of the haiden]] '''Tsukiyomi Shrine''' (つきよみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Kokubuhigashi-fure, Ashibe-chō, {{ill|lt=Iki|Q861519|WD=Q861519}}, {{ill|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|Q169376|WD=Q169376}}. {{ill|lt=Tachibana Mitsuyoshi|Q11544184|WD=Q11544184}} identified it as an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}), but this is considered incorrectAshibe Town History Editorial Committee, ''Ashibe Town History'' (Ashibe Town, 1978). === Enshrined deities === The current enshrined deities are three: {{ill|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto, Tsukuyumi-no-Mikoto, Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}}. All three are the same deity. The original deity was a “mountain deity,” but after Tachibana Mitsuyoshi’s identification, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} came to be regarded as the enshrined deity. === History === Its origin before {{ill|lt=Enpō|Q1145735|WD=Q1145735}} is unknown. In Enpō 4 ({{ill|lt=1676|Q7628|WD=Q7628}}), Tachibana Mitsuyoshi, who investigated Shikinaisha on {{ill|lt=Iki Province|Q1052938|WD=Q1052938|lt=Iki Island}} by order of the {{ill|lt=Hirado Domain|Q3034453|WD=Q3034453}}, identified this shrine as “Tsukiyomi Shrine,” a Shikinaisha myōjin taisha. On June 1 of the same year, domain lord {{ill|lt=Matsura Shigenobu|Q11531889|WD=Q11531889|lt=Matsura Shigenobu}} dedicated a stone shrine and one wooden mirror as {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532}}, and it was treated as a Shikinaisha for some time. Before that, however, it had no notable ritual facilities and was simply called “Yama no Kami” (mountain god). Mitsuyoshi had identified it as a Shikinaisha because the place was called “Kiyotsuki,” but it was also called “Fukatsuki,” whose origin is thought to be “fukafuchi”''Iki no Kuni Jinjaki''.. Therefore Mitsuyoshi’s judgment is considered mistaken, and the original Tsukiyomi Shrine is held to be {{ill|lt=Hakozaki Hachiman Shrine (Iki)|Q111442063|WD=Q111442063|lt=Hakozaki Hachiman Shrine}}. It is said that one of the “Tsukinobe stones” (moon-delay stones), which {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803}} is said to have placed against her abdomen during the {{ill|lt=Conquest of the Three Han|Q11249435|WD=Q11249435}} to delay childbirth, was dedicated here, but its whereabouts are now unknown. At present, local residents including {{ill|lt=ujiko|Q125562175|WD=Q125562175}} and worshippers from outside the island maintain and improve the shrine buildings and grounds. === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === *''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'', Vol. 24, Saikaidō, Kōgakkan University Press, Shōwa 53 {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つきよみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsukiyomi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsukiyomi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsukiyomi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iki Island]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:長崎県の神社]] [[Category:壱岐市の歴史]] [[Category:長崎県の旧無格社]] [[Category:壱岐市の建築物]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Tsukiyomi-jinja (Iki)}} [[JA:月讀神社 (壱岐市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11516219}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Tsukiyomi Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:Iki Tukiyomi shrine.JPG|250px|Tsukiyomi Shrine]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|Q169376|WD=Q169376}}{{ill|lt=Iki|Q861519|WD=Q861519}} Ashibe-chō Kokubuhigashi-fure 464 |位置 = {{Coord|33|47|58.69|N|129|43|25.35|E|region:JP-42_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsukiyomi Shrine}} |祭神 = Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto
Tsukuyumi-no-Mikoto
Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto |社格 = candidate shrine for Shikinaisha major shrine (myōjin major) / former unranked shrine |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = October 23 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagasaki|label=Tsukiyomi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[ファイル:Iki tsukiyomishrine haiden.jpg|サムネイル|Distant view of the haiden]] '''Tsukiyomi Shrine''' (つきよみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Kokubuhigashi-fure, Ashibe-chō, {{ill|lt=Iki|Q861519|WD=Q861519}}, {{ill|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|Q169376|WD=Q169376}}. {{ill|lt=Tachibana Mitsuyoshi|Q11544184|WD=Q11544184}} identified it as an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}), but this is considered incorrectAshibe Town History Editorial Committee, ''Ashibe Town History'' (Ashibe Town, 1978). === Enshrined deities === The current enshrined deities are three: {{ill|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto, Tsukuyumi-no-Mikoto, Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}}. All three are the same deity. The original deity was a “mountain deity,” but after Tachibana Mitsuyoshi’s identification, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} came to be regarded as the enshrined deity. === History === Its origin before {{ill|lt=Enpō|Q1145735|WD=Q1145735}} is unknown. In Enpō 4 ({{ill|lt=1676|Q7628|WD=Q7628}}), Tachibana Mitsuyoshi, who investigated Shikinaisha on {{ill|lt=Iki Province|Q1052938|WD=Q1052938|lt=Iki Island}} by order of the {{ill|lt=Hirado Domain|Q3034453|WD=Q3034453}}, identified this shrine as “Tsukiyomi Shrine,” a Shikinaisha myōjin taisha. On June 1 of the same year, domain lord {{ill|lt=Matsura Shigenobu|Q11531889|WD=Q11531889|lt=Matsura Shigenobu}} dedicated a stone shrine and one wooden mirror as {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532}}, and it was treated as a Shikinaisha for some time. Before that, however, it had no notable ritual facilities and was simply called “Yama no Kami” (mountain god). Mitsuyoshi had identified it as a Shikinaisha because the place was called “Kiyotsuki,” but it was also called “Fukatsuki,” whose origin is thought to be “fukafuchi”''Iki no Kuni Jinjaki''.. Therefore Mitsuyoshi’s judgment is considered mistaken, and the original Tsukiyomi Shrine is held to be {{ill|lt=Hakozaki Hachiman Shrine (Iki)|Q111442063|WD=Q111442063|lt=Hakozaki Hachiman Shrine}}. It is said that one of the “Tsukinobe stones” (moon-delay stones), which {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803}} is said to have placed against her abdomen during the {{ill|lt=Conquest of the Three Han|Q11249435|WD=Q11249435}} to delay childbirth, was dedicated here, but its whereabouts are now unknown. At present, local residents including {{ill|lt=ujiko|Q125562175|WD=Q125562175}} and worshippers from outside the island maintain and improve the shrine buildings and grounds. === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === *''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'', Vol. 24, Saikaidō, Kōgakkan University Press, Shōwa 53 {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つきよみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsukiyomi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsukiyomi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsukiyomi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iki Island]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:長崎県の神社]] [[Category:壱岐市の歴史]] [[Category:長崎県の旧無格社]] [[Category:壱岐市の建築物]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Shrine|WD=Q135041492}} ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|lt=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '長崎県壱岐市芦辺町国分東触464', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '長崎県壱岐市芦辺町箱崎釘ノ尾触', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Tsukiyomi-jinja (Iki)}} [[JA:月讀神社 (壱岐市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11516219}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Tsukiyomi Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:Iki Tukiyomi shrine.JPG|250px|Tsukiyomi Shrine]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|Q169376|WD=Q169376}}{{ill|lt=Iki|Q861519|WD=Q861519}} Ashibe-chō Kokubuhigashi-fure 464 |位置 = {{Coord|33|47|58.69|N|129|43|25.35|E|region:JP-42_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsukiyomi Shrine}} |祭神 = Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto
Tsukuyumi-no-Mikoto
Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto |社格 = candidate shrine for Shikinaisha major shrine (myōjin major) / former unranked shrine |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = October 23 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagasaki|label=Tsukiyomi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[ファイル:Iki tsukiyomishrine haiden.jpg|サムネイル|Distant view of the haiden]] '''Tsukiyomi Shrine''' (つきよみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Kokubuhigashi-fure, Ashibe-chō, {{ill|lt=Iki|Q861519|WD=Q861519}}, {{ill|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|Q169376|WD=Q169376}}. {{ill|lt=Tachibana Mitsuyoshi|Q11544184|WD=Q11544184}} identified it as an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}), but this is considered incorrectAshibe Town History Editorial Committee, ''Ashibe Town History'' (Ashibe Town, 1978). === Enshrined deities === The current enshrined deities are three: {{ill|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto, Tsukuyumi-no-Mikoto, Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}}. All three are the same deity. The original deity was a “mountain deity,” but after Tachibana Mitsuyoshi’s identification, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} came to be regarded as the enshrined deity. === History === Its origin before {{ill|lt=Enpō|Q1145735|WD=Q1145735}} is unknown. In Enpō 4 ({{ill|lt=1676|Q7628|WD=Q7628}}), Tachibana Mitsuyoshi, who investigated Shikinaisha on {{ill|lt=Iki Province|Q1052938|WD=Q1052938|lt=Iki Island}} by order of the {{ill|lt=Hirado Domain|Q3034453|WD=Q3034453}}, identified this shrine as “Tsukiyomi Shrine,” a Shikinaisha myōjin taisha. On June 1 of the same year, domain lord {{ill|lt=Matsura Shigenobu|Q11531889|WD=Q11531889|lt=Matsura Shigenobu}} dedicated a stone shrine and one wooden mirror as {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532}}, and it was treated as a Shikinaisha for some time. Before that, however, it had no notable ritual facilities and was simply called “Yama no Kami” (mountain god). Mitsuyoshi had identified it as a Shikinaisha because the place was called “Kiyotsuki,” but it was also called “Fukatsuki,” whose origin is thought to be “fukafuchi”''Iki no Kuni Jinjaki''.. Therefore Mitsuyoshi’s judgment is considered mistaken, and the original Tsukiyomi Shrine is held to be {{ill|lt=Hakozaki Hachiman Shrine (Iki)|Q111442063|WD=Q111442063|lt=Hakozaki Hachiman Shrine}}. It is said that one of the “Tsukinobe stones” (moon-delay stones), which {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803}} is said to have placed against her abdomen during the {{ill|lt=Conquest of the Three Han|Q11249435|WD=Q11249435}} to delay childbirth, was dedicated here, but its whereabouts are now unknown. At present, local residents including {{ill|lt=ujiko|Q125562175|WD=Q125562175}} and worshippers from outside the island maintain and improve the shrine buildings and grounds. === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === *''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'', Vol. 24, Saikaidō, Kōgakkan University Press, Shōwa 53 {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つきよみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsukiyomi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsukiyomi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsukiyomi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iki Island]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:長崎県の神社]] [[Category:壱岐市の歴史]] [[Category:長崎県の旧無格社]] [[Category:壱岐市の建築物]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Tsukiyomi-jinja (Iki)}} [[JA:月讀神社 (壱岐市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11516219}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Tsukiyomi Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:Iki Tukiyomi shrine.JPG|250px|Tsukiyomi Shrine]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|Q169376|WD=Q169376}}{{ill|lt=Iki|Q861519|WD=Q861519}} Ashibe-chō Kokubuhigashi-fure 464 |位置 = {{Coord|33|47|58.69|N|129|43|25.35|E|region:JP-42_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsukiyomi Shrine}} |祭神 = Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto
Tsukuyumi-no-Mikoto
Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto |社格 = candidate shrine for Shikinaisha major shrine (myōjin major) / former unranked shrine |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = October 23 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagasaki|label=Tsukiyomi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[ファイル:Iki tsukiyomishrine haiden.jpg|サムネイル|Distant view of the haiden]] '''Tsukiyomi Shrine''' (つきよみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Kokubuhigashi-fure, Ashibe-chō, {{ill|lt=Iki|Q861519|WD=Q861519}}, {{ill|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|Q169376|WD=Q169376}}. {{ill|lt=Tachibana Mitsuyoshi|Q11544184|WD=Q11544184}} identified it as an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}), but this is considered incorrectAshibe Town History Editorial Committee, ''Ashibe Town History'' (Ashibe Town, 1978). === Enshrined deities === The current enshrined deities are three: {{ill|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto, Tsukuyumi-no-Mikoto, Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}}. All three are the same deity. The original deity was a “mountain deity,” but after Tachibana Mitsuyoshi’s identification, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} came to be regarded as the enshrined deity. === History === Its origin before {{ill|lt=Enpō|Q1145735|WD=Q1145735}} is unknown. In Enpō 4 ({{ill|lt=1676|Q7628|WD=Q7628}}), Tachibana Mitsuyoshi, who investigated Shikinaisha on {{ill|lt=Iki Province|Q1052938|WD=Q1052938|lt=Iki Island}} by order of the {{ill|lt=Hirado Domain|Q3034453|WD=Q3034453}}, identified this shrine as “Tsukiyomi Shrine,” a Shikinaisha myōjin taisha. On June 1 of the same year, domain lord {{ill|lt=Matsura Shigenobu|Q11531889|WD=Q11531889|lt=Matsura Shigenobu}} dedicated a stone shrine and one wooden mirror as {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532}}, and it was treated as a Shikinaisha for some time. Before that, however, it had no notable ritual facilities and was simply called “Yama no Kami” (mountain god). Mitsuyoshi had identified it as a Shikinaisha because the place was called “Kiyotsuki,” but it was also called “Fukatsuki,” whose origin is thought to be “fukafuchi”''Iki no Kuni Jinjaki''.. Therefore Mitsuyoshi’s judgment is considered mistaken, and the original Tsukiyomi Shrine is held to be {{ill|lt=Hakozaki Hachiman Shrine (Iki)|Q111442063|WD=Q111442063|lt=Hakozaki Hachiman Shrine}}. It is said that one of the “Tsukinobe stones” (moon-delay stones), which {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803}} is said to have placed against her abdomen during the {{ill|lt=Conquest of the Three Han|Q11249435|WD=Q11249435}} to delay childbirth, was dedicated here, but its whereabouts are now unknown. At present, local residents including {{ill|lt=ujiko|Q125562175|WD=Q125562175}} and worshippers from outside the island maintain and improve the shrine buildings and grounds. === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === *''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'', Vol. 24, Saikaidō, Kōgakkan University Press, Shōwa 53 {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つきよみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsukiyomi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsukiyomi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsukiyomi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iki Island]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:長崎県の神社]] [[Category:壱岐市の歴史]] [[Category:長崎県の旧無格社]] [[Category:壱岐市の建築物]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Shrine|WD=Q135041492|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}} ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '長崎県壱岐市芦辺町国分東触464', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '長崎県壱岐市芦辺町箱崎釘ノ尾触', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Tsukiyomi-jinja (Iki)}} [[JA:月讀神社 (壱岐市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11516219}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Tsukiyomi Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:Iki Tukiyomi shrine.JPG|250px|Tsukiyomi Shrine]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|Q169376|WD=Q169376|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Iki|Q861519|WD=Q861519|1=Iki, Nagasaki}} Ashibe-chō Kokubuhigashi-fure 464 |位置 = {{Coord|33|47|58.69|N|129|43|25.35|E|region:JP-42_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsukiyomi Shrine}} |祭神 = Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto
Tsukuyumi-no-Mikoto
Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto |社格 = candidate shrine for Shikinaisha major shrine (myōjin major) / former unranked shrine |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = October 23 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagasaki|label=Tsukiyomi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[ファイル:Iki tsukiyomishrine haiden.jpg|サムネイル|Distant view of the haiden]] '''Tsukiyomi Shrine''' (つきよみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kokubuhigashi-fure, Ashibe-chō, {{ill|lt=Iki|Q861519|WD=Q861519|1=Iki, Nagasaki}}, {{ill|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|Q169376|WD=Q169376|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}}. {{ill|lt=Tachibana Mitsuyoshi|Q11544184|WD=Q11544184|1=Tachibana Mitsuyoshi}} identified it as an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), but this is considered incorrectAshibe Town History Editorial Committee, ''Ashibe Town History'' (Ashibe Town, 1978). === Enshrined deities === The current enshrined deities are three: {{ill|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto, Tsukuyumi-no-Mikoto, Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}}. All three are the same deity. The original deity was a “mountain deity,” but after Tachibana Mitsuyoshi’s identification, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}} came to be regarded as the enshrined deity. === History === Its origin before {{ill|lt=Enpō|Q1145735|WD=Q1145735|1=Enpō}} is unknown. In Enpō 4 ({{ill|lt=1676|Q7628|WD=Q7628|1=1676}}), Tachibana Mitsuyoshi, who investigated Shikinaisha on {{ill|lt=Iki Province|Q1052938|WD=Q1052938|lt=Iki Island|1=Iki Province}} by order of the {{ill|lt=Hirado Domain|Q3034453|WD=Q3034453|1=Hirado Domain}}, identified this shrine as “Tsukiyomi Shrine,” a Shikinaisha myōjin taisha. On June 1 of the same year, domain lord {{ill|lt=Matsura Shigenobu|Q11531889|WD=Q11531889|lt=Matsura Shigenobu|1=Matsura Shigenobu}} dedicated a stone shrine and one wooden mirror as {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532|1=Shintai}}, and it was treated as a Shikinaisha for some time. Before that, however, it had no notable ritual facilities and was simply called “Yama no Kami” (mountain god). Mitsuyoshi had identified it as a Shikinaisha because the place was called “Kiyotsuki,” but it was also called “Fukatsuki,” whose origin is thought to be “fukafuchi”''Iki no Kuni Jinjaki''.. Therefore Mitsuyoshi’s judgment is considered mistaken, and the original Tsukiyomi Shrine is held to be {{ill|lt=Hakozaki Hachiman Shrine (Iki)|Q111442063|WD=Q111442063|lt=Hakozaki Hachiman Shrine|1=Hakozaki Hachiman Shrine}}. It is said that one of the “Tsukinobe stones” (moon-delay stones), which {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} is said to have placed against her abdomen during the {{ill|lt=Conquest of the Three Han|Q11249435|WD=Q11249435|1=Three Han Campaign}} to delay childbirth, was dedicated here, but its whereabouts are now unknown. At present, local residents including {{ill|lt=ujiko|Q125562175|WD=Q125562175|1=Ujiko}} and worshippers from outside the island maintain and improve the shrine buildings and grounds. === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === *''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'', Vol. 24, Saikaidō, Kōgakkan University Press, Shōwa 53 {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つきよみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsukiyomi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsukiyomi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsukiyomi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iki Island]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:長崎県の神社]] [[Category:壱岐市の歴史]] [[Category:長崎県の旧無格社]] [[Category:壱岐市の建築物]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 8310005005848 == Commons category (P373) == * Tsukiyomi-jinja (Iki) == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Tsukuyomi Shrine|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi Shrine|WD=Q135041492|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}} ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '長崎県壱岐市芦辺町国分東触464', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '長崎県壱岐市芦辺町箱崎釘ノ尾触', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Tsukiyomi-jinja (Iki)}} [[JA:月讀神社 (壱岐市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11516219}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Tsukiyomi Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:Iki Tukiyomi shrine.JPG|250px|Tsukiyomi Shrine]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|Q169376|WD=Q169376|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Iki|Q861519|WD=Q861519|1=Iki, Nagasaki}} Ashibe-chō Kokubuhigashi-fure 464 |位置 = {{Coord|33|47|58.69|N|129|43|25.35|E|region:JP-42_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsukiyomi Shrine}} |祭神 = Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto
Tsukuyumi-no-Mikoto
Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto |社格 = candidate shrine for Shikinaisha major shrine (myōjin major) / former unranked shrine |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = October 23 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagasaki|label=Tsukiyomi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[ファイル:Iki tsukiyomishrine haiden.jpg|サムネイル|Distant view of the haiden]] '''Tsukiyomi Shrine''' (つきよみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kokubuhigashi-fure, Ashibe-chō, {{ill|lt=Iki|Q861519|WD=Q861519|1=Iki, Nagasaki}}, {{ill|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|Q169376|WD=Q169376|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}}. {{ill|lt=Tachibana Mitsuyoshi|Q11544184|WD=Q11544184|1=Tachibana Mitsuyoshi}} identified it as an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), but this is considered incorrectAshibe Town History Editorial Committee, ''Ashibe Town History'' (Ashibe Town, 1978). === Enshrined deities === The current enshrined deities are three: {{ill|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto, Tsukuyumi-no-Mikoto, Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}}. All three are the same deity. The original deity was a “mountain deity,” but after Tachibana Mitsuyoshi’s identification, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}} came to be regarded as the enshrined deity. === History === Its origin before {{ill|lt=Enpō|Q1145735|WD=Q1145735|1=Enpō}} is unknown. In Enpō 4 ({{ill|lt=1676|Q7628|WD=Q7628|1=1676}}), Tachibana Mitsuyoshi, who investigated Shikinaisha on {{ill|lt=Iki Province|Q1052938|WD=Q1052938|lt=Iki Island|1=Iki Province}} by order of the {{ill|lt=Hirado Domain|Q3034453|WD=Q3034453|1=Hirado Domain}}, identified this shrine as “Tsukiyomi Shrine,” a Shikinaisha myōjin taisha. On June 1 of the same year, domain lord {{ill|lt=Matsura Shigenobu|Q11531889|WD=Q11531889|lt=Matsura Shigenobu|1=Matsura Shigenobu}} dedicated a stone shrine and one wooden mirror as {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532|1=Shintai}}, and it was treated as a Shikinaisha for some time. Before that, however, it had no notable ritual facilities and was simply called “Yama no Kami” (mountain god). Mitsuyoshi had identified it as a Shikinaisha because the place was called “Kiyotsuki,” but it was also called “Fukatsuki,” whose origin is thought to be “fukafuchi”''Iki no Kuni Jinjaki''.. Therefore Mitsuyoshi’s judgment is considered mistaken, and the original Tsukiyomi Shrine is held to be {{ill|lt=Hakozaki Hachiman Shrine (Iki)|Q111442063|WD=Q111442063|lt=Hakozaki Hachiman Shrine|1=Hakozaki Hachiman Shrine}}. It is said that one of the “Tsukinobe stones” (moon-delay stones), which {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} is said to have placed against her abdomen during the {{ill|lt=Conquest of the Three Han|Q11249435|WD=Q11249435|1=Three Han Campaign}} to delay childbirth, was dedicated here, but its whereabouts are now unknown. At present, local residents including {{ill|lt=ujiko|Q125562175|WD=Q125562175|1=Ujiko}} and worshippers from outside the island maintain and improve the shrine buildings and grounds. === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === *''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'', Vol. 24, Saikaidō, Kōgakkan University Press, Shōwa 53 {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つきよみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsukiyomi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsukiyomi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsukiyomi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iki Island]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:長崎県の神社]] [[Category:壱岐市の歴史]] [[Category:長崎県の旧無格社]] [[Category:壱岐市の建築物]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 8310005005848 == Commons category (P373) == * Tsukiyomi-jinja (Iki) == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Tsukuyomi Shrine|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi Shrine|WD=Q135041492|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}} ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '長崎県壱岐市芦辺町国分東触464', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '長崎県壱岐市芦辺町箱崎釘ノ尾触', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Tsukiyomi-jinja (Iki)}} [[JA:月讀神社 (壱岐市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11516219}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Tsukiyomi Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:Iki Tukiyomi shrine.JPG|250px|Tsukiyomi Shrine]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|Q169376|WD=Q169376|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Iki|Q861519|WD=Q861519|1=Iki, Nagasaki}} Ashibe-chō Kokubuhigashi-fure 464 |位置 = {{Coord|33|47|58.69|N|129|43|25.35|E|region:JP-42_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsukiyomi Shrine}} |祭神 = Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto
Tsukuyumi-no-Mikoto
Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto |社格 = candidate shrine for Shikinaisha major shrine (myōjin major) / former unranked shrine |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = October 23 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagasaki|label=Tsukiyomi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[ファイル:Iki tsukiyomishrine haiden.jpg|サムネイル|Distant view of the haiden]] '''Tsukiyomi Shrine''' (つきよみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kokubuhigashi-fure, Ashibe-chō, {{ill|lt=Iki|Q861519|WD=Q861519|1=Iki, Nagasaki}}, {{ill|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|Q169376|WD=Q169376|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}}. {{ill|lt=Tachibana Mitsuyoshi|Q11544184|WD=Q11544184|1=Tachibana Mitsuyoshi}} identified it as an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), but this is considered incorrectAshibe Town History Editorial Committee, ''Ashibe Town History'' (Ashibe Town, 1978). === Enshrined deities === The current enshrined deities are three: {{ill|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto, Tsukuyumi-no-Mikoto, Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}}. All three are the same deity. The original deity was a “mountain deity,” but after Tachibana Mitsuyoshi’s identification, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}} came to be regarded as the enshrined deity. === History === Its origin before {{ill|lt=Enpō|Q1145735|WD=Q1145735|1=Enpō}} is unknown. In Enpō 4 ({{ill|lt=1676|Q7628|WD=Q7628|1=1676}}), Tachibana Mitsuyoshi, who investigated Shikinaisha on {{ill|lt=Iki Province|Q1052938|WD=Q1052938|lt=Iki Island|1=Iki Province}} by order of the {{ill|lt=Hirado Domain|Q3034453|WD=Q3034453|1=Hirado Domain}}, identified this shrine as “Tsukiyomi Shrine,” a Shikinaisha myōjin taisha. On June 1 of the same year, domain lord {{ill|lt=Matsura Shigenobu|Q11531889|WD=Q11531889|lt=Matsura Shigenobu|1=Matsura Shigenobu}} dedicated a stone shrine and one wooden mirror as {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532|1=Shintai}}, and it was treated as a Shikinaisha for some time. Before that, however, it had no notable ritual facilities and was simply called “Yama no Kami” (mountain god). Mitsuyoshi had identified it as a Shikinaisha because the place was called “Kiyotsuki,” but it was also called “Fukatsuki,” whose origin is thought to be “fukafuchi”''Iki no Kuni Jinjaki''.. Therefore Mitsuyoshi’s judgment is considered mistaken, and the original Tsukiyomi Shrine is held to be {{ill|lt=Hakozaki Hachiman Shrine (Iki)|Q111442063|WD=Q111442063|lt=Hakozaki Hachiman Shrine|1=Hakozaki Hachiman Shrine}}. It is said that one of the “Tsukinobe stones” (moon-delay stones), which {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} is said to have placed against her abdomen during the {{ill|lt=Conquest of the Three Han|Q11249435|WD=Q11249435|1=Three Han Campaign}} to delay childbirth, was dedicated here, but its whereabouts are now unknown. At present, local residents including {{ill|lt=ujiko|Q125562175|WD=Q125562175|1=Ujiko}} and worshippers from outside the island maintain and improve the shrine buildings and grounds. === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === *''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'', Vol. 24, Saikaidō, Kōgakkan University Press, Shōwa 53 {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つきよみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsukiyomi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsukiyomi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsukiyomi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iki Island]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:長崎県の神社]] [[Category:壱岐市の歴史]] [[Category:長崎県の旧無格社]] [[Category:壱岐市の建築物]][[Category:qq]]

Shrine|WD=Q135041492|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}} ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '長崎県壱岐市芦辺町国分東触464', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '長崎県壱岐市芦辺町箱崎釘ノ尾触', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Tsukiyomi-jinja (Iki)}} [[JA:月讀神社 (壱岐市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11516219}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Tsukiyomi Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:Iki Tukiyomi shrine.JPG|250px|Tsukiyomi Shrine]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|Q169376|WD=Q169376|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Iki|Q861519|WD=Q861519|1=Iki, Nagasaki}} Ashibe-chō Kokubuhigashi-fure 464 |位置 = {{Coord|33|47|58.69|N|129|43|25.35|E|region:JP-42_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsukiyomi Shrine}} |祭神 = Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto
Tsukuyumi-no-Mikoto
Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto |社格 = candidate shrine for Shikinaisha major shrine (myōjin major) / former unranked shrine |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = October 23 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagasaki|label=Tsukiyomi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[ファイル:Iki tsukiyomishrine haiden.jpg|サムネイル|Distant view of the haiden]] '''Tsukiyomi Shrine''' (つきよみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kokubuhigashi-fure, Ashibe-chō, {{ill|lt=Iki|Q861519|WD=Q861519|1=Iki, Nagasaki}}, {{ill|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|Q169376|WD=Q169376|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}}. {{ill|lt=Tachibana Mitsuyoshi|Q11544184|WD=Q11544184|1=Tachibana Mitsuyoshi}} identified it as an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), but this is considered incorrectAshibe Town History Editorial Committee, ''Ashibe Town History'' (Ashibe Town, 1978). === Enshrined deities === The current enshrined deities are three: {{ill|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto, Tsukuyumi-no-Mikoto, Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}}. All three are the same deity. The original deity was a “mountain deity,” but after Tachibana Mitsuyoshi’s identification, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}} came to be regarded as the enshrined deity. === History === Its origin before {{ill|lt=Enpō|Q1145735|WD=Q1145735|1=Enpō}} is unknown. In Enpō 4 ({{ill|lt=1676|Q7628|WD=Q7628|1=1676}}), Tachibana Mitsuyoshi, who investigated Shikinaisha on {{ill|lt=Iki Province|Q1052938|WD=Q1052938|lt=Iki Island|1=Iki Province}} by order of the {{ill|lt=Hirado Domain|Q3034453|WD=Q3034453|1=Hirado Domain}}, identified this shrine as “Tsukiyomi Shrine,” a Shikinaisha myōjin taisha. On June 1 of the same year, domain lord {{ill|lt=Matsura Shigenobu|Q11531889|WD=Q11531889|lt=Matsura Shigenobu|1=Matsura Shigenobu}} dedicated a stone shrine and one wooden mirror as {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532|1=Shintai}}, and it was treated as a Shikinaisha for some time. Before that, however, it had no notable ritual facilities and was simply called “Yama no Kami” (mountain god). Mitsuyoshi had identified it as a Shikinaisha because the place was called “Kiyotsuki,” but it was also called “Fukatsuki,” whose origin is thought to be “fukafuchi”''Iki no Kuni Jinjaki''.. Therefore Mitsuyoshi’s judgment is considered mistaken, and the original Tsukiyomi Shrine is held to be {{ill|lt=Hakozaki Hachiman Shrine (Iki)|Q111442063|WD=Q111442063|lt=Hakozaki Hachiman Shrine|1=Hakozaki Hachiman Shrine}}. It is said that one of the “Tsukinobe stones” (moon-delay stones), which {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} is said to have placed against her abdomen during the {{ill|lt=Conquest of the Three Han|Q11249435|WD=Q11249435|1=Three Han Campaign}} to delay childbirth, was dedicated here, but its whereabouts are now unknown. At present, local residents including {{ill|lt=ujiko|Q125562175|WD=Q125562175|1=Ujiko}} and worshippers from outside the island maintain and improve the shrine buildings and grounds. === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === *''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'', Vol. 24, Saikaidō, Kōgakkan University Press, Shōwa 53 {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsukiyomi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsukiyomi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsukiyomi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iki Island]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:長崎県の神社]] [[Category:壱岐市の歴史]] [[Category:長崎県の旧無格社]] [[Category:壱岐市の建築物]][[Category:qq]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 8310005005848 == Commons category (P373) == * Tsukiyomi-jinja (Iki) == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Tsukuyomi Shrine|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi Shrine|WD=Q135041492|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}} ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '長崎県壱岐市芦辺町国分東触464', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '長崎県壱岐市芦辺町箱崎釘ノ尾触', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Tsukiyomi-jinja (Iki)}} [[JA:月讀神社 (壱岐市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11516219}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Tsukiyomi Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:Iki Tukiyomi shrine.JPG|250px|Tsukiyomi Shrine]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|Q169376|WD=Q169376|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Iki|Q861519|WD=Q861519|1=Iki, Nagasaki}} Ashibe-chō Kokubuhigashi-fure 464 |位置 = {{Coord|33|47|58.69|N|129|43|25.35|E|region:JP-42_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsukiyomi Shrine}} |祭神 = Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto
Tsukuyumi-no-Mikoto
Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto |社格 = candidate shrine for Shikinaisha major shrine (myōjin major) / former unranked shrine |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = October 23 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagasaki|label=Tsukiyomi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[ファイル:Iki tsukiyomishrine haiden.jpg|サムネイル|Distant view of the haiden]] '''Tsukiyomi Shrine''' (つきよみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kokubuhigashi-fure, Ashibe-chō, {{ill|lt=Iki|Q861519|WD=Q861519|1=Iki, Nagasaki}}, {{ill|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|Q169376|WD=Q169376|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}}. {{ill|lt=Tachibana Mitsuyoshi|Q11544184|WD=Q11544184|1=Tachibana Mitsuyoshi}} identified it as an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), but this is considered incorrectAshibe Town History Editorial Committee, ''Ashibe Town History'' (Ashibe Town, 1978). === Enshrined deities === The current enshrined deities are three: {{ill|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto, Tsukuyumi-no-Mikoto, Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}}. All three are the same deity. The original deity was a “mountain deity,” but after Tachibana Mitsuyoshi’s identification, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}} came to be regarded as the enshrined deity. === History === Its origin before {{ill|lt=Enpō|Q1145735|WD=Q1145735|1=Enpō}} is unknown. In Enpō 4 ({{ill|lt=1676|Q7628|WD=Q7628|1=1676}}), Tachibana Mitsuyoshi, who investigated Shikinaisha on {{ill|lt=Iki Province|Q1052938|WD=Q1052938|lt=Iki Island|1=Iki Province}} by order of the {{ill|lt=Hirado Domain|Q3034453|WD=Q3034453|1=Hirado Domain}}, identified this shrine as “Tsukiyomi Shrine,” a Shikinaisha myōjin taisha. On June 1 of the same year, domain lord {{ill|lt=Matsura Shigenobu|Q11531889|WD=Q11531889|lt=Matsura Shigenobu|1=Matsura Shigenobu}} dedicated a stone shrine and one wooden mirror as {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532|1=Shintai}}, and it was treated as a Shikinaisha for some time. Before that, however, it had no notable ritual facilities and was simply called “Yama no Kami” (mountain god). Mitsuyoshi had identified it as a Shikinaisha because the place was called “Kiyotsuki,” but it was also called “Fukatsuki,” whose origin is thought to be “fukafuchi”''Iki no Kuni Jinjaki''.. Therefore Mitsuyoshi’s judgment is considered mistaken, and the original Tsukiyomi Shrine is held to be {{ill|lt=Hakozaki Hachiman Shrine (Iki)|Q111442063|WD=Q111442063|lt=Hakozaki Hachiman Shrine|1=Hakozaki Hachiman Shrine}}. It is said that one of the “Tsukinobe stones” (moon-delay stones), which {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} is said to have placed against her abdomen during the {{ill|lt=Conquest of the Three Han|Q11249435|WD=Q11249435|1=Three Han Campaign}} to delay childbirth, was dedicated here, but its whereabouts are now unknown. At present, local residents including {{ill|lt=ujiko|Q125562175|WD=Q125562175|1=Ujiko}} and worshippers from outside the island maintain and improve the shrine buildings and grounds. === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === *''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'', Vol. 24, Saikaidō, Kōgakkan University Press, Shōwa 53 {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsukiyomi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsukiyomi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsukiyomi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iki Island]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:長崎県の神社]] [[Category:壱岐市の歴史]] [[Category:長崎県の旧無格社]] [[Category:壱岐市の建築物]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 8310005005848 == Commons category (P373) == * Tsukiyomi-jinja (Iki) == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Tsukuyomi Shrine|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi Shrine|WD=Q135041492|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}} ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '長崎県壱岐市芦辺町国分東触464', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '長崎県壱岐市芦辺町箱崎釘ノ尾触', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Tsukiyomi-jinja (Iki)}} [[JA:月讀神社 (壱岐市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11516219}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Tsukiyomi Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:Iki Tukiyomi shrine.JPG|250px|Tsukiyomi Shrine]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|Q169376|WD=Q169376|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Iki|Q861519|WD=Q861519|1=Iki, Nagasaki}} Ashibe-chō Kokubuhigashi-fure 464 |位置 = {{Coord|33|47|58.69|N|129|43|25.35|E|region:JP-42_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsukiyomi Shrine}} |祭神 = Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto
Tsukuyumi-no-Mikoto
Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto |社格 = candidate shrine for Shikinaisha major shrine (myōjin major) / former unranked shrine |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = October 23 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagasaki|label=Tsukiyomi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[ファイル:Iki tsukiyomishrine haiden.jpg|サムネイル|Distant view of the haiden]] '''Tsukiyomi Shrine''' (つきよみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kokubuhigashi-fure, Ashibe-chō, {{ill|lt=Iki|Q861519|WD=Q861519|1=Iki, Nagasaki}}, {{ill|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|Q169376|WD=Q169376|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}}. {{ill|lt=Tachibana Mitsuyoshi|Q11544184|WD=Q11544184|1=Tachibana Mitsuyoshi}} identified it as an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), but this is considered incorrectAshibe Town History Editorial Committee, ''Ashibe Town History'' (Ashibe Town, 1978). === Enshrined deities === The current enshrined deities are three: {{ill|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto, Tsukuyumi-no-Mikoto, Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}}. All three are the same deity. The original deity was a “mountain deity,” but after Tachibana Mitsuyoshi’s identification, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}} came to be regarded as the enshrined deity. === History === Its origin before {{ill|lt=Enpō|Q1145735|WD=Q1145735|1=Enpō}} is unknown. In Enpō 4 ({{ill|lt=1676|Q7628|WD=Q7628|1=1676}}), Tachibana Mitsuyoshi, who investigated Shikinaisha on {{ill|lt=Iki Province|Q1052938|WD=Q1052938|lt=Iki Island|1=Iki Province}} by order of the {{ill|lt=Hirado Domain|Q3034453|WD=Q3034453|1=Hirado Domain}}, identified this shrine as “Tsukiyomi Shrine,” a Shikinaisha myōjin taisha. On June 1 of the same year, domain lord {{ill|lt=Matsura Shigenobu|Q11531889|WD=Q11531889|lt=Matsura Shigenobu|1=Matsura Shigenobu}} dedicated a stone shrine and one wooden mirror as {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532|1=Shintai}}, and it was treated as a Shikinaisha for some time. Before that, however, it had no notable ritual facilities and was simply called “Yama no Kami” (mountain god). Mitsuyoshi had identified it as a Shikinaisha because the place was called “Kiyotsuki,” but it was also called “Fukatsuki,” whose origin is thought to be “fukafuchi”''Iki no Kuni Jinjaki''.. Therefore Mitsuyoshi’s judgment is considered mistaken, and the original Tsukiyomi Shrine is held to be {{ill|lt=Hakozaki Hachiman Shrine (Iki)|Q111442063|WD=Q111442063|lt=Hakozaki Hachiman Shrine|1=Hakozaki Hachiman Shrine}}. It is said that one of the “Tsukinobe stones” (moon-delay stones), which {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} is said to have placed against her abdomen during the {{ill|lt=Conquest of the Three Han|Q11249435|WD=Q11249435|1=Three Han Campaign}} to delay childbirth, was dedicated here, but its whereabouts are now unknown. At present, local residents including {{ill|lt=ujiko|Q125562175|WD=Q125562175|1=Ujiko}} and worshippers from outside the island maintain and improve the shrine buildings and grounds. === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === *''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'', Vol. 24, Saikaidō, Kōgakkan University Press, Shōwa 53 {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsukiyomi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsukiyomi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsukiyomi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iki Island]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:長崎県の神社]] [[Category:壱岐市の歴史]] [[Category:Former Unranked Shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:壱岐市の建築物]]

{'text': '長崎県壱岐市芦辺町箱崎釘ノ尾触', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Tsukiyomi-jinja (Iki)}} [[JA:月讀神社 (壱岐市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11516219}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Tsukiyomi Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:Iki Tukiyomi shrine.JPG|250px|Tsukiyomi Shrine]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|Q169376|WD=Q169376|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Iki|Q861519|WD=Q861519|1=Iki, Nagasaki}} Ashibe-chō Kokubuhigashi-fure 464 |位置 = {{Coord|33|47|58.69|N|129|43|25.35|E|region:JP-42_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsukiyomi Shrine}} |祭神 = Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto
Tsukuyumi-no-Mikoto
Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto |社格 = candidate shrine for Shikinaisha major shrine (myōjin major) / former unranked shrine |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = October 23 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagasaki|label=Tsukiyomi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[ファイル:Iki tsukiyomishrine haiden.jpg|サムネイル|Distant view of the haiden]] '''Tsukiyomi Shrine''' (つきよみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kokubuhigashi-fure, Ashibe-chō, {{ill|lt=Iki|Q861519|WD=Q861519|1=Iki, Nagasaki}}, {{ill|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|Q169376|WD=Q169376|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}}. {{ill|lt=Tachibana Mitsuyoshi|Q11544184|WD=Q11544184|1=Tachibana Mitsuyoshi}} identified it as an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), but this is considered incorrectAshibe Town History Editorial Committee, ''Ashibe Town History'' (Ashibe Town, 1978). === Enshrined deities === The current enshrined deities are three: {{ill|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto, Tsukuyumi-no-Mikoto, Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}}. All three are the same deity. The original deity was a “mountain deity,” but after Tachibana Mitsuyoshi’s identification, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}} came to be regarded as the enshrined deity. === History === Its origin before {{ill|lt=Enpō|Q1145735|WD=Q1145735|1=Enpō}} is unknown. In Enpō 4 ({{ill|lt=1676|Q7628|WD=Q7628|1=1676}}), Tachibana Mitsuyoshi, who investigated Shikinaisha on {{ill|lt=Iki Province|Q1052938|WD=Q1052938|lt=Iki Island|1=Iki Province}} by order of the {{ill|lt=Hirado Domain|Q3034453|WD=Q3034453|1=Hirado Domain}}, identified this shrine as “Tsukiyomi Shrine,” a Shikinaisha myōjin taisha. On June 1 of the same year, domain lord {{ill|lt=Matsura Shigenobu|Q11531889|WD=Q11531889|lt=Matsura Shigenobu|1=Matsura Shigenobu}} dedicated a stone shrine and one wooden mirror as {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532|1=Shintai}}, and it was treated as a Shikinaisha for some time. Before that, however, it had no notable ritual facilities and was simply called “Yama no Kami” (mountain god). Mitsuyoshi had identified it as a Shikinaisha because the place was called “Kiyotsuki,” but it was also called “Fukatsuki,” whose origin is thought to be “fukafuchi”''Iki no Kuni Jinjaki''.. Therefore Mitsuyoshi’s judgment is considered mistaken, and the original Tsukiyomi Shrine is held to be {{ill|lt=Hakozaki Hachiman Shrine (Iki)|Q111442063|WD=Q111442063|lt=Hakozaki Hachiman Shrine|1=Hakozaki Hachiman Shrine}}. It is said that one of the “Tsukinobe stones” (moon-delay stones), which {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} is said to have placed against her abdomen during the {{ill|lt=Conquest of the Three Han|Q11249435|WD=Q11249435|1=Three Han Campaign}} to delay childbirth, was dedicated here, but its whereabouts are now unknown. At present, local residents including {{ill|lt=ujiko|Q125562175|WD=Q125562175|1=Ujiko}} and worshippers from outside the island maintain and improve the shrine buildings and grounds. === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === *''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'', Vol. 24, Saikaidō, Kōgakkan University Press, Shōwa 53 {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsukiyomi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsukiyomi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsukiyomi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iki Island]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:壱岐市の歴史]] [[Category:Former Unranked Shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:壱岐市の建築物]]

{'text': '長崎県壱岐市芦辺町箱崎釘ノ尾触', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Tsukiyomi-jinja (Iki)}} [[JA:月讀神社 (壱岐市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11516219}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Tsukiyomi Shrine |画像 = [[ファイル:Iki Tukiyomi shrine.JPG|250px|Tsukiyomi Shrine]]
Torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|Q169376|WD=Q169376|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Iki|Q861519|WD=Q861519|1=Iki, Nagasaki}} Ashibe-chō Kokubuhigashi-fure 464 |位置 = {{Coord|33|47|58.69|N|129|43|25.35|E|region:JP-42_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tsukiyomi Shrine}} |祭神 = Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto
Tsukuyumi-no-Mikoto
Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto |社格 = candidate shrine for Shikinaisha major shrine (myōjin major) / former unranked shrine |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = October 23 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagasaki|label=Tsukiyomi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[ファイル:Iki tsukiyomishrine haiden.jpg|サムネイル|Distant view of the haiden]] '''Tsukiyomi Shrine''' (つきよみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kokubuhigashi-fure, Ashibe-chō, {{ill|lt=Iki|Q861519|WD=Q861519|1=Iki, Nagasaki}}, {{ill|lt=Nagasaki Prefecture|Q169376|WD=Q169376|1=Nagasaki Prefecture}}. {{ill|lt=Tachibana Mitsuyoshi|Q11544184|WD=Q11544184|1=Tachibana Mitsuyoshi}} identified it as an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), but this is considered incorrectAshibe Town History Editorial Committee, ''Ashibe Town History'' (Ashibe Town, 1978). === Enshrined deities === The current enshrined deities are three: {{ill|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto, Tsukuyumi-no-Mikoto, Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}}. All three are the same deity. The original deity was a “mountain deity,” but after Tachibana Mitsuyoshi’s identification, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}} came to be regarded as the enshrined deity. === History === Its origin before {{ill|lt=Enpō|Q1145735|WD=Q1145735|1=Enpō}} is unknown. In Enpō 4 ({{ill|lt=1676|Q7628|WD=Q7628|1=1676}}), Tachibana Mitsuyoshi, who investigated Shikinaisha on {{ill|lt=Iki Province|Q1052938|WD=Q1052938|lt=Iki Island|1=Iki Province}} by order of the {{ill|lt=Hirado Domain|Q3034453|WD=Q3034453|1=Hirado Domain}}, identified this shrine as “Tsukiyomi Shrine,” a Shikinaisha myōjin taisha. On June 1 of the same year, domain lord {{ill|lt=Matsura Shigenobu|Q11531889|WD=Q11531889|lt=Matsura Shigenobu|1=Matsura Shigenobu}} dedicated a stone shrine and one wooden mirror as {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532|1=Shintai}}, and it was treated as a Shikinaisha for some time. Before that, however, it had no notable ritual facilities and was simply called “Yama no Kami” (mountain god). Mitsuyoshi had identified it as a Shikinaisha because the place was called “Kiyotsuki,” but it was also called “Fukatsuki,” whose origin is thought to be “fukafuchi”''Iki no Kuni Jinjaki''.. Therefore Mitsuyoshi’s judgment is considered mistaken, and the original Tsukiyomi Shrine is held to be {{ill|lt=Hakozaki Hachiman Shrine (Iki)|Q111442063|WD=Q111442063|lt=Hakozaki Hachiman Shrine|1=Hakozaki Hachiman Shrine}}. It is said that one of the “Tsukinobe stones” (moon-delay stones), which {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} is said to have placed against her abdomen during the {{ill|lt=Conquest of the Three Han|Q11249435|WD=Q11249435|1=Three Han Campaign}} to delay childbirth, was dedicated here, but its whereabouts are now unknown. At present, local residents including {{ill|lt=ujiko|Q125562175|WD=Q125562175|1=Ujiko}} and worshippers from outside the island maintain and improve the shrine buildings and grounds. === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === *''Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku'', Vol. 24, Saikaidō, Kōgakkan University Press, Shōwa 53 {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tsukiyomi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsukiyomi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tsukiyomi Shrine]][[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iki Island]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:壱岐市の歴史]] [[Category:Former Unranked Shrines in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:壱岐市の建築物]]
